masthead.tagline: Overcast at 87, high of 93, and a 28 percent chance the sky does anything about it.

lead.headline: Third US Soldier Killed as Iran Strikes Widen
lead.blurb: A US service member died in Iraq during the controlled detonation of a downed Iranian drone, a day after two troops were killed in Jordan, and Washington answered with fresh airstrikes on Iran. Iranian missiles fired toward Jordan now risk pulling Israel into the exchange.

secondaries.headline: Torres Breaks Deadlock in Extra Time of World Cup Final
secondaries.blurb: Ferran Torres scored in extra time to put Spain ahead of a ten-man Argentina at the New York New Jersey Stadium. Trump watched from the stands under heavy security.

secondaries.headline: Burnham Takes Over as Britain's Seventh PM in a Decade
secondaries.blurb: The Labour leader enters Downing Street on Monday promising "more breathing room" and fresh backing for the North Sea. Thames Water creditors are already drafting a legal challenge to any nationalisation.

secondaries.headline: Russia Fires 41 Missiles and 125 Drones at Ukraine
secondaries.blurb: Eight people were killed and dozens injured as several cities were struck overnight, with Kyiv the primary target. Ukraine continued its own campaign against Russian infrastructure.

secondaries.headline: Anthropic Keeps Fable 5 in Max and Team Plans
secondaries.blurb: From July 20 Fable 5 stays bundled at half limits for Max and Team Premium, with Pro users getting a one-time $100 credit. Competitive pressure from GPT-5.6 Sol made removing it untenable.

briefs.headline: Apple Retakes Most-Valuable-Company Title From Nvidia
Ryan Fox Birdies the 72nd to Win The Open
Dozens Missing After Ferry Sinks Off Guyana
Moonshot Halts New Signups Under Kimi K3 Load
Nolan's Odyssey Opens to $264 Million Worldwide
Thunder Send Lu Dort to Hawks in Three-Team Deal
Apple Music Rises to $11.99 in Latest Price Round
Builder Replaces $120,000 Bowling System With ESP32s
Tate Brothers Arrested in Miami as UK Charges Reach 59
Peru Earthquakes Kill Six, Displace Hundreds

markets.note: Crypto barely moved, with bitcoin flat near $64,800 and ether up 0.6% to $1,873.

stories.headline: Brain Implant Restores Touch to Paralyzed Man
stories.blurb: The recipient can now feed himself, drink from a cup, and feel his dog's fur. It is the clearest consumer-legible demonstration yet of restored sensation, not just movement.

stories.headline: DNA From Trash Closes a 45-Year-Old Murder Case
stories.blurb: A 79-year-old man was arrested after investigators matched samples pulled from his discarded rubbish. Genetic cold-case work keeps compressing decades into weeks.

stories.headline: KAIST and Stanford Build a Suit That Dresses You
stories.blurb: The robotic rig fits a person in roughly ten seconds. The obvious application is assistive care, not the Iron Man comparison the demo invites.

stories.headline: Nvidia CEO's Leather Jacket Sells for Nearly $1 Million
stories.blurb: The garment went for just under seven figures at a New York auction. A useful gauge of where the AI trade sits culturally.

stories.headline: World Cup's Hatsune Miku Curse Reaches the Final
stories.blurb: The mascot has passed from Japan to Brazil, Norway, England and Argentina, each losing 2-1 after taking her on. Spain now has a claim.

stories.headline: Shushy Leads This Week's Trending TikTok Sounds
stories.blurb: The prodshushy track tops a short list ahead of 0landrys and CK Music. A thin chart by recent standards.

stories.headline: Dort Traded to Hawks in Three-Team Deal
stories.blurb: Oklahoma City sent the All-Defensive wing to Atlanta for three second-round picks, with Zaccharie Risacher going to Dallas and Ryan Nembhard to the Hawks. Atlanta buys defense; the Thunder bank flexibility.

stories.headline: Padres Hang 19 on Royals as Cubs Rout Twins
stories.blurb: San Diego hit five home runs in a 19-2 demolition in Kansas City, while Shota Imanaga threw seven shutout innings in a 10-1 Cubs win. Atlanta beat Texas 8-5.

stories.headline: Barkley and Family Unhurt in Attempted Home Burglary
stories.blurb: Tredyffrin Township police responded to an attempted break-in at the Eagles running back's home Saturday morning. No one was injured.

stories.headline: Knicks Edge Spurs 94-90 in Summer Action
stories.blurb: New York held off San Antonio in the day's only NBA result. A quiet scoreboard in a loud offseason.

stories.headline: Fable 5 Stays in Max and Team Subscriptions
stories.blurb: Anthropic reverses course from July 20, keeping Fable 5 bundled at 50% of limits for Max and Team Premium. Pro and Team Standard fall back to usage credits plus a one-time $100 grant.

stories.headline: Consultant Says AI Mania Is Hollowing Out Corporate Strategy
stories.blurb: Nik Suresh's account of enterprise AI adoption includes an executive who wrote an AI-centered strategy for a $2B company without ever having used a chatbot. The anecdotes are anonymous but the pattern is familiar.

stories.headline: Claude Code Ships on the Rust Rewrite of Bun
stories.blurb: Simon Willison confirmed via strings on his own binary that Claude Code v2.1.181 onward runs Bun's Rust port. Startup got 10% faster on Linux and nobody noticed.

stories.headline: Browser Tool Explains SQLite Query Plans
stories.blurb: A Pyodide-hosted SQLite runs EXPLAIN and EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN in the browser and annotates the output. Useful if you have ever stared blankly at a query plan.

stories.headline: Perforce Charges $500 for AI-Narrated Training Videos
stories.blurb: A Hacker News thread piles on the vendor for selling synthetic-voice courseware at enterprise prices. The complaint is less about the AI than the invoice.

stories.headline: Quixote Web Framework Gets a Commit After 21 Years
stories.blurb: The Python framework's repository, whose first commit was a Subversion import two decades ago, saw activity this week. A small note in favor of software that refuses to die.

stories.headline: Another US Troop Killed as Strikes on Iran Continue
stories.blurb: A service member died in Iraq during the controlled detonation of a downed Iranian drone, bringing the week's American toll to three. Iranian missiles aimed at Jordan have raised the odds of Israeli involvement.

stories.headline: Overnight Russian Barrage Kills Eight in Ukraine
stories.blurb: Forty-one missiles and 125 drones struck several cities, with Kyiv the focus. Ukrainian forces continued strikes on Russian infrastructure.

stories.headline: Burnham Enters Downing Street Promising Breathing Room
stories.blurb: Britain's seventh prime minister in ten years takes office Monday, shaped by the north-south divide he campaigned on as Manchester mayor. North Sea support is expected on day one.

stories.headline: Guyana Ferry Sinking Leaves Dozens Unaccounted For
stories.blurb: Sixty-seven of 116 aboard have been rescued, including 15 children, after the vessel went down late Saturday. Officials call it one of the country's worst maritime disasters.

stories.headline: Thames Water Creditors Ready Challenge to Nationalisation
stories.blurb: Lenders would pursue full repayment of multi-billion-pound debts if the new government takes the utility into public hands. The fight would set the price of every future nationalisation.

stories.headline: Tate Brothers Arrested in US as Charges Reach 59
stories.blurb: The Crown Prosecution Service has requested extradition after adding further UK charges. Their lawyer maintains they are innocent.

stories.headline: Twin Earthquakes in Peru Kill Six
stories.blurb: Quakes in the Andes region killed at least six and injured more than twenty, displacing hundreds. Damage assessments in mountain communities are still incomplete.

stories.headline: Inspections Find Repeated Violence in Dementia Care
stories.blurb: Court records and facility inspections show nursing homes failing to prevent assaults between residents, some fatal. Regulators had flagged the same gaps for years.

stories.headline: Xbox Promises More Exclusives, Warns Against Fixating on Single-Player
stories.blurb: Executives say Gears of War: E-Day and Clockwork Revolution are not the whole slate, while nudging expectations away from solo campaigns. The message continues Xbox's habit of clarifying itself into confusion.

stories.headline: The Odyssey Opens to $264 Million Worldwide
stories.blurb: Nolan's adaptation beat every projection in its first weekend. Studios will read it as proof that giving a director real budget and real freedom still pays.

stories.headline: Xbox Still Won't Say If Fallout 5 Is Console Exclusive
stories.blurb: Bethesda announced the new Fallout titles without clarifying platform exclusivity. That silence is itself the strategy.

stories.headline: Avatar, Halo and Splatoon All Land July 23
stories.blurb: Three major releases collide on a single Thursday, the busiest gaming day of the summer. Somebody's sales chart is going to suffer.

stories.headline: Game of Thrones Strategy Game Slips to Early 2027
stories.blurb: War for Westeros has been delayed, extending the franchise's long run of disappointing games. The genre fit, at least, is right this time.

stories.headline: Counter-Strike 2 Tops 1.27 Million Concurrent Players
stories.blurb: Valve's shooter again led Steam's peak chart ahead of PUBG and Dota 2, with Slay the Spire 2 holding 196,000. Steam Machine sits atop the top-sellers list.

stories.headline: Madonna and BTS Headline First World Cup Halftime Show
stories.blurb: The eleven-minute set also featured Shakira, Justin Bieber, Burna Boy and Chris Martin, with the Muppets in support. With Trump in attendance, NPR read the spectacle as more political than intended.

stories.headline: L7 Bassist Jennifer Finch Dies at 59
stories.blurb: The grunge musician died Saturday after a diagnosis of aggressive brain cancer. She anchored one of the defining bands of the early-nineties LA scene.

stories.headline: Tour Cancellations Mount as Ticket Prices Climb 37%
stories.blurb: Stereogum reports widespread undersold shows across the 2026 US season, with average top-100 tour prices up 37.2% since 2019. Quebec's FEQ is held up as the counterexample.

stories.headline: Tomahawk Play First Show in Thirteen Years
stories.blurb: Mike Patton's experimental supergroup returned to the stage after a 2013 hiatus. No word yet on a full tour.

stories.headline: Ella Langley and Drake Split the Apple Music Top Five
stories.blurb: Langley holds the number one with "Choosin' Texas" and lands three songs in the top seven, while Drake takes second and sixth. Morgan Wallen and Lil Baby fill out the rest.

stories.headline: Apple Briefly Passes Nvidia as Most Valuable Company
stories.blurb: Apple's valuation neared $5 trillion after a 20% run since late June, edging past Nvidia intraday before the two traded places again. The gap is now a rounding error.

stories.headline: Apple Raises Prices on Music, iCloud+ and Japanese iPhones
stories.blurb: Apple Music individual is now $11.99 and Family $19.99, following worldwide Mac and iPad increases last month. iCloud+ rose in eight countries and Japanese iPhone prices climbed up to 11%.

stories.headline: Moonshot Suspends Signups Under Kimi K3 Demand
stories.blurb: The Chinese lab stopped taking new subscriptions after its latest model overwhelmed capacity. Compute scarcity remains the real constraint on frontier releases.

stories.headline: ESP32 Rig Replaces a $120,000 Bowling Center System
stories.blurb: A builder rebuilt an entire alley's scoring and pinsetter control for about $1,600 in microcontrollers. The thread is a clinic in reverse-engineering ancient industrial hardware.

stories.headline: Apple and Justice Department Open Settlement Talks
stories.blurb: Apple has floated several offers in the 2024 antitrust suit over iPhone platform lock-in. Early talks are not a deal, but they are a change of posture.

stories.headline: San Francisco Demands Apple and Google Pull Nudify Apps
stories.blurb: Cease-and-desist letters target 13 face-swap apps used to generate nonconsensual nude images, eight of them on the App Store. The city wants the stores to stop taking a cut of in-app payments.

stories.headline: OpenAI Cuts Codex Context Window to 272k Tokens
stories.blurb: The reduction from 372k drew a large and unhappy Hacker News thread. Context length is now a headline spec, and shrinking it reads as a cost decision.

stories.headline: Last MPEG-4 Visual Patent Has Expired
stories.blurb: The final patent covering the codec lapsed, putting a long-licensed format fully in the clear. Mostly of archival interest, but a real milestone for open tooling.
